intent: PawPulse is a hyper-localized social ecosystem for pet owners that utilizes real-time geolocation to facilitate impromptu playdates, share verified health records, and offer a community-driven marketplace for pet-specific services. The core workflow involves creating a comprehensive pet profile, syncing with existing veterinary EHR systems, and utilizing an interactive 'Park-Radar' map to connect with nearby owners based on compatibility traits, such as energy level, breed size, and behavioral temperament.

entities.name: PetProfileRegistry
entities.description: A centralized store for comprehensive pet metadata, behavioral temperaments, and health vitals synced via veterinary EHR integrations.

entities.name: GeoSpatialNode
entities.description: A dynamic index of real-time user coordinates managed by an in-memory spatial database to support the Park-Radar proximity queries.

entities.name: HealthVerificationAudit
entities.description: An immutable record container for certified vaccine documentation and wellness snapshots retrieved from external veterinary systems.

entities.name: PlaydateEventCoordinator
entities.description: A state machine managing the lifecycle of an impromptu meet-up, handling WebSocket-based notifications and participant handshakes.

entities.name: ServiceMarketplaceLedger
entities.description: An accounting entity facilitating service discovery and escrow-protected financial transactions between pet owners and vetted service providers.

entities.name: PrivacyConsentManifest
entities.description: A granular configuration object tracking user data access permissions and GDPR/CCPA compliance states for each metadata category.

architecture.description: The architecture employs a microservices pattern where the PetProfileRegistry serves as the single source of truth. Upon user login via OAuth, the PrivacyConsentManifest initializes a secure session. The Park-Radar functionality utilizes a dedicated GeoSpatialNode that streams real-time updates via WebSockets for low-latency notifications. When a user requests a playdate, the PlaydateEventCoordinator initiates a transactional workflow, verifying health status via the HealthVerificationAudit before pushing alert packets to the recipient. All financial interactions are routed through the ServiceMarketplaceLedger, ensuring escrow-based trust. High-resolution media is offloaded to cloud object storage, with signed URLs generated at the API gateway layer to ensure secure, privacy-compliant access. The entire ecosystem is deployed within a Kubernetes cluster with cross-region replication to satisfy high-availability and regional data residency requirements.

endpoints.summary: Retrieve certified health and vaccine records

validation_rules: recipient must have verified health audit

endpoints.summary: Create a new escrow-protected service transaction

rules.description: Enforce health verification status before allowing playdate initiation
rules.logic: If PlaydateEventCoordinator attempts to initiate a session, verify that HealthVerificationAudit contains a non-expired vaccination record for the participating pet.

rules.description: Restrict spatial data exposure based on privacy consent levels
rules.logic: Before GeoSpatialNode broadcasts location updates, validate against PrivacyConsentManifest to ensure the user has granted proximity sharing permissions.

rules.description: Ensure transactional integrity for escrow-protected payments
rules.logic: ServiceMarketplaceLedger must move funds to escrow status upon booking confirmation and only release to the service provider after the event state transitions to 'Completed' in the PlaydateEventCoordinator.

rules.description: Validate data residency and GDPR/CCPA compliance on session initialization
rules.logic: PrivacyConsentManifest must perform a lookup upon OAuth login to restrict data access to the specific regional shards permitted by the user's geographic residency flag.

rules.description: Maintain sync consistency for pet health vitals
rules.logic: PetProfileRegistry must trigger a reconciliation job if the HealthVerificationAudit snapshot does not match the latest timestamped record from the integrated veterinary EHR.
